code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Helper to clean numeric or quoted objects
    def _clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip('"')
        # Strip surrounding whitespace
        o = o.strip()
        # If numeric, format nicely
        if o.replace(".", "", 1).isdigit():
            # Convert to int if no fractional part
            if o.endswith(".0"):
                o = o[:-2]
            try:
                num = int(o)
                o = f"{num:,}"
            except ValueError:
                pass
        return o

    # Ordinal mapping for runway descriptors
    _ordinal_map = {
        "1st": "first",
        "2nd": "second",
        "3rd": "third",
        "4th": "fourth",
        "5th": "fifth",
    }

    # Templates for direct predicates
    templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "{subject}'s runway length is {obj} metres",
        "runwayName": "has a runway named {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has IATA code {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has a runway surface of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "the length of the 1st runway at {subject} is {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "the length of the 1st runway at {subject} is {obj} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "the 1st runway number at {subject} is {obj}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "the length of the 4th runway at {subject} is {obj} feet",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "the 5th runway number at {subject} is {obj}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "the 5th runway surface at {subject} is {obj}",
        # Specific handling for runway surface type predicates with ordinals
        # e.g., "1stRunwaySurfaceType" → "the surface of the first runway at {subject} is {obj}"
        # The actual rendering is performed later in the loop.
        # Additional taxonomic predicates
        "order": "is of the order {obj}",
        "class": "is of the class {obj}",
        "division": "is of the division {obj}",
        "family": "is of the family {obj}",
        # Fallback generic template
        "default": "{predicate} {obj}"
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            obj = _clean_obj(t.object)
            if tmpl:
                parts.append(tmpl.format(subject=subject, obj=obj))
            else:
                # Fallback generic phrase
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                parts.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
        # Combine parts into one sentence for this subject
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {parts[0]}."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Join all generated sentences; if there is only one, return it without extra spaces
    return " ".join(sentences).strip()

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
